description_long: This is a roughly rectangular, upright stone with a coarse, uneven surface and a natural, weathered appearance. The front face features a prominent vertical groove running down its center, suggesting either natural erosion or intentional shaping. The stone's sides are irregular, with one side showing a deep indentation or cavity, while the back is relatively flat and unmarked. Its color is a muted, earthy brown with subtle variations and occasional lighter patches, likely from mineral deposits or surface wear. The texture is rugged and granular, with visible pitting and small protrusions typical of unpolished stone. This object would be well-suited as a boundary marker, obstacle, or decorative element in an urban park or along a pathway.
description: A tall, rough, brown stone with a vertical groove on its front and an irregular, weathered surface. Its sides are uneven, with one deep indentation and subtle color variations.
description_view_0: The front view shows a vertical groove running down the center of the stone, with a rugged, pitted surface and some lighter patches.
description_view_1: The side view reveals a deep indentation or cavity, emphasizing the stone's irregular, natural shape.
description_view_2: The back view is relatively flat and featureless, with a consistent rough texture and uniform brown coloration.
description_view_3: The opposite side view highlights the stone's elongated form and uneven surface, with less pronounced indentations than the other side.
attribute_location: urban park boundary or pathway edge
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature, partially embedded in the ground
attribute_pattern: Irregular pitting and a single vertical groove; no artificial carvings or regular patterns
attribute_weathering: Rough and eroded, with visible surface pitting and minor discoloration from exposure
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with paved surfaces but may blend with natural landscaping or other stone features
